findings.finding: Risk audit mitigation for 'model not found' says 'model-not-found → error JSON, not crash'. The constraint at lines 59-65 explicitly lists model-not-found as a condition requiring status=skipped + exit 2. An implementer following the risk audit will produce status=error for model-not-found, blocking convergence. This is the same failure class as EC-1 (fixed in round 1), reintroduced via a stale risk-audit entry that was not updated during the round-2 fix pass.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the mitigation column for the HIGH 'gpt-5.5 model ID' risk row from 'model-not-found → error JSON, not crash' to 'model-not-found → status=skipped + exit 2 (treated identically to auth failure per constraint at §Threat model); TLMFORGE_OPENAI_MODEL env override available for model ID flexibility'.

findings.finding: Risk audit LOW row for TOCTOU says 'Results in status=error instead of status=skipped; correct graceful behavior'. This explicitly labels status=error for a provider-failure condition as 'correct' — directly contradicting the constraint that ALL LLM provider failures must produce status=skipped + exit 2 (lines 59-65). A TDD implementer reading this will deliberately implement the TOCTOU path to produce status=error. A code reviewer reading this will accept status=error in the TOCTOU path as 'by design'. Both outcomes violate the user requirement.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the LOW TOCTOU row mitigation with: 'The API call is wrapped in the global openai.APIError handler; auth failure discovered at call time → status=skipped + exit 2, identical to the pre-flight check result. No special-case handling needed for this race window.'

findings.finding: Phase 1 test at line 249: 'All critical findings in error output have suggested_fix (string, len >= 8)'. After the NEC-1 fix, status=error is only emitted for disk-full implementation bugs, not provider failures. status=skipped JSON carries findings=[]. The assertion 'critical findings in error output' is ambiguous — if it targets status=error JSON it is untestable in unit tests; if it targets skipped JSON the assertion vacuously passes but could mask a regression where status=error is incorrectly emitted with findings. The test description should be precise.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with two precise assertions: (1) 'status=skipped JSON always has findings=[]'; (2) 'status=ok JSON where any finding has severity=critical must have suggested_fix as non-empty string with len >= 8'.
